app.name: RocketMQ-Rust
app.one_liner: A high-performance, reliable, and feature-rich unofficial Rust implementation of Apache RocketMQ, designed to bring enterprise-grade message middleware to the Rust ecosystem.

screens.name: Dashboard Overview
screens.purpose: Provide operators with real-time visibility into cluster health, message throughput, and component status.

rust_commands.name: publish_message
rust_commands.purpose: Send a message to a specified topic using the RocketMQ-Rust client with full protocol compatibility.

rust_commands.name: run_controller_mode
rust_commands.purpose: Initialize the controller mode added in v0.7.0 for managing cluster coordination without relying on ZooKeeper.

rust_commands.name: start_proxy_mode
rust_commands.purpose: Launch the proxy mode component introduced in v0.8.0 to enable flexible routing and protocol translation.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement basic message production and consumption using async Rust
week 1: Integrate RocketMQResult error handling pattern from recent PRs
week 1: Set up modular crate structure mirroring rocketmq-broker, rocketmq-client, and rocketmq-namesrv
week 2: Add proxy mode support as per v0.8.0 release notes
week 2: Configure CI workflows matching GitHub Actions badges
week 2: Implement dashboard UI scaffolding using provided architecture diagram
acceptance_tests: All components (broker, proxy, dashboard) start successfully using published crate binaries.
Controller mode initializes and maintains cluster state without external dependencies like ZooKeeper.
Proxy mode routes messages correctly between clients and brokers without data loss.
The system can produce and consume messages with full Apache RocketMQ protocol compatibility.
